responsibility_assigned_to: hxqwen14b v5 CUDA forward link wiring
claimed_blocker: ALM r11 EXITED with RC_ERR_CUDA_TODO(-5) on libhxqwen14b.so

actual_blocker_diagnosis.root_cause: build_hxqwen14b_linux.hexa never invoked nvcc on hxqwen14b_cuda.cu — only fed gcc the .c file. With HXQWEN14B_CUDA=1 the resulting .so would have undefined references for hxqwen14b_cu_launch_* and v5.4 base_forward path, OR (in stub mode) silently lack any cuLaunchKernel symbol so the watchdog probe rejects it forever.
actual_blocker_diagnosis.kernel_implementation_status: ALREADY COMPLETE in self/native/hxqwen14b_cuda.cu (608 LOC v5.3 + v5.4) and self/native/hxqwen14b.c (3872 LOC). 11 CUDA kernels + 6 host launchers + full 48-layer base_forward_v54 + load_all_weights orchestrator + ce_loss_v54 + smoke_v54.
actual_blocker_diagnosis.trainer_call_path: train_alm_lora.hexa forward_qwen14b_with_lora is itself a hexa-side stub returning synthetic logits — it never crosses FFI to forward_with_lora. The only real FFI calls are hxqwen14b_load (slot probe), version, free. So r11 BLOCKED_FFI was triggered by either (a) load returning <=0 (missing weights / wrong path) or (b) the watchdog cuLaunchKernel symbol probe failing on a stub-built .so.
actual_blocker_diagnosis.what_v5_cuda_was_actually_missing: Just the link recipe — nvcc compile of .cu and combined link into .so.

symbol_verification.watchdog_probe_added: post-link nm -D | grep cudaLaunchKernel — emits PRESENT/MISSING line so the build itself reports whether r11_ffi_watchdog probe will pass

mac_xverify_dylib.interpretation: hxqwen14b.c (with all v5.4 additions) compiles cleanly with HXQWEN14B_CUDA undefined on Mac clang

mac_native_dylib.interpretation: Mac runtime FFI surface intact; trainer dry-run on Mac will get version=52, load_all_weights=-5 (RC_ERR_CUDA_TODO as designed for non-CUDA builds)

linux_cuda_so.reason: nvcc + cuBLAS + cudart only present on H100 pod; Mac cannot exercise. Build script verified syntactically correct via mac-xverify path. Pod-side run-of-recipe deferred to first invocation on H100 (hxqwen14b_cuda.cu compile error visibility deferred to nvcc on pod).
linux_cuda_so.next_action_for_pod: ssh pod && cd /workspace/hexa-lang && HXQWEN14B_CUDA=1 ./hexa run tool/build_hxqwen14b_linux.hexa  # produces self/native/build/libhxqwen14b.so with all 51 symbols

alm_r11_unblock_chain.step_a_build_so: On H100 pod, run the updated build script with HXQWEN14B_CUDA=1. Expected outputs: 34 host symbols + 17 CUDA symbols + cudaLaunchKernel PRESENT.
alm_r11_unblock_chain.step_b_watchdog_passes_probe: r11_ffi_watchdog.hexa nm -D probe will see cuLaunchKernel and proceed to spawn_trainer.
alm_r11_unblock_chain.step_d_trainer_loop_runs: forward_qwen14b_with_lora returns synthetic-logit array (NOT empty), so check_ffi_blocked stays false; train_loop emits step=1/500 line and watchdog exits 0.
alm_r11_unblock_chain.step_e_real_training_quality: Loss is currently moved by stub forward only — A/B grads via softmax_ce_grad over 16-token stub_vocab. To get real Qwen2.5 base loss, future commit must wire forward_qwen14b_with_lora to call hxqwen14b_load_all_weights + hxqwen14b_base_forward_v54 + hxqwen14b_ce_loss_v54 (the v5.4 entries this build now exposes). Out of scope for THIS commit.

completed_kernels.name: v53_rope_kernel
completed_kernels.purpose: RoPE rotate_half (HF Qwen2 layout, v5.4.3 fixed)

completed_kernels.name: v54_add_bias_1d_kernel
completed_kernels.purpose: QKV bias broadcast (Qwen2.5 ships QKV biases)

completed_host_launchers: hxqwen14b_cu_launch_sgemm_rowmajor_xwt (cuBLAS Sgemm wrapper, v5.4.4 layout fix)

missing_kernels_or_steps.name: v54 backward kernels (rmsnorm/rope/gqa/swiglu)
missing_kernels_or_steps.owner: future v6 work — base frozen, only LoRA backward needed which v5 host-side already covers

missing_kernels_or_steps.name: train_alm_lora.hexa forward wiring to base_forward_v54
missing_kernels_or_steps.blocker: yes for real loss, no for r11 boot
missing_kernels_or_steps.note: trainer currently uses hexa-side synthetic forward; real CE requires rewiring forward_qwen14b_with_lora to FFI-call load_all_weights + base_forward_v54 + ce_loss_v54 + apply LoRA delta inline. Recommend separate commit so this build can be exercised independently.

missing_kernels_or_steps.name: fp32 lm_head materialization for 14B
missing_kernels_or_steps.note: 14B lm_head fp32 = 152064*5120*4 = 3.1GB; H100 80GB has headroom. v5.4 base_forward_v54 already allocates this.

next_steps.action: ssh into ALM r11 H100 pod (or fresh pod), git pull hexa-lang, run HXQWEN14B_CUDA=1 ./hexa run tool/build_hxqwen14b_linux.hexa
Verify all 34+17 symbols PRESENT and cudaLaunchKernel PRESENT in produced libhxqwen14b.so
Touch /workspace/ckpt_alm14b_r11_*/r11_ffi_watchdog.cuda_landed to signal watchdog (or simply wait — symbol probe will pass on next poll)
Watchdog spawns trainer; train_alm_lora.hexa runs to step=1; watchdog exits 0
Future PR: wire forward_qwen14b_with_lora to v5.4 FFI for real Qwen2.5-14B base loss + LoRA delta — this is the next true blocker for r12 quality, not r11 boot

rationalisation_check_d1_root_cause.did_we_fill_the_stub: yes — the stubs were already filled by prior agents (a48c754f6c52a490a authored hxqwen14b_cuda.cu with 11 kernels). What was missing was just the link recipe in build_hxqwen14b_linux.hexa to actually pull those kernels into the .so. Without the recipe, the kernels existed in source but weren't in any built artifact, so r11 would always BLOCKED_FFI.
rationalisation_check_d1_root_cause.did_we_make_a_fallback: no — we did not introduce a CPU-only fallback or mock CUDA path. The only thing that changed is the recipe. The kernels themselves are real cuBLAS Sgemm + hand-rolled CUDA.
rationalisation_check_d1_root_cause.would_a_pod_build_now_unblock_r11: yes for the watchdog probe + trainer boot. The trainer's hexa-side synthetic forward keeps step=1+ flowing; full Qwen2.5 base loss requires the follow-up wiring listed in next_steps[5].

v8_safe_commit_note: single .hexa file edit, no .py/.rs/.sh introduced, no L0 freeze touched, no R37 violation
